---
title: CISA ScubaGear (M365 SCuBA Assessor) — Adapter Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: scubagear
adapter-class: conformance
mission-class: policy
status: active
phase: phase-1
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-04-14'
---

# CISA ScubaGear (M365 SCuBA Assessor) — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `active` · **Class:** `conformance` · **Mission:** `policy` · **Phase:** `phase-1`

**Canon source:** `canon/adapter-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The CISA ScubaGear Adapter is the **most mature conformance adapter** in the UIAO ecosystem and the canonical SCuBA assessor on the M365 surface. As a policy-class conformance adapter, it wraps CISA's [ScubaGear](https://github.com/cisagov/ScubaGear) tool to evaluate Microsoft 365 tenant configuration against the SCuBA Secure Configuration Baselines, producing normalized, KSI-anchored assessment findings.

The adapter is strictly **read-only** — it observes and assesses the M365 tenant state without modifying it. It pairs with the [`scuba`](../scuba/scuba.html) modernization adapter (which applies the baseline) to form the canonical **assess-then-remediate** cycle that anchors continuous M365 posture management in the UIAO substrate.

Three primary functions:

1. **Seven-workload SCuBA assessment.** Runs ScubaGear against AAD, Defender, Exchange Online, PowerBI, Power Platform, SharePoint, and Teams. The PowerShell tool produces a structured JSON findings artifact and an HTML report; this adapter parses both into the canonical claim shape.
2. **OPA/Rego policy evaluation.** Each ScubaGear finding is evaluated against the OPA/Rego policy pack that maps SCuBA baseline statements to UIAO KSIs. 150+ policy-to-control mappings translate "SCuBA setting X" into "evidence for NIST 800-53 control Y".
3. **Deterministic OSCAL emission.** The dedicated IR pipeline (`scuba/transform.py`, `ir/adapters/scuba/normalize` + `transformer`) produces an `EvidenceBundle` suitable for direct `build_sar()` consumption — the most complete end-to-end OSCAL output in the adapter family.

**Implementation:** Most mature adapter in the repo. Uses a dedicated IR pipeline (`scuba/transform.py`, 384 lines) under `ir/adapters/scuba/`, not the generic `DatabaseAdapterBase` pattern. Pinned to ScubaGear v1.5.1 with a monthly policy-pin bump treated as a Routine Recurring Change per CONMON.md §6.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `aad`, `defender`, `exo`, `powerbi`, `powerplatform`, `sharepoint`, `teams`

**Reads:** ScubaGear PowerShell assessment output (`findings.json`, `metadata.json`, `scubagear-report.html`, `scubagear-report.json`), OPA/Rego policy evaluation results, plus the tenant configuration that ScubaGear itself reads via Microsoft Graph and the workload-specific PowerShell modules. Requires the `powershell-7.4` runtime on the runner.

**Emits:** KSI-mapped `EvidenceObject` records, `DriftState` records, an `EvidenceBundle` consumable directly by `build_sar()`, plus the `findings.json`, `metadata.json`, and HTML report passed through from ScubaGear for assessor review. The most complete OSCAL pipeline in the ecosystem.

**Does NOT:** modify M365 tenant configuration (that is [`scuba`](../scuba/scuba.html)'s role), execute arbitrary PowerShell beyond the documented ScubaGear invocation, or access tenant data beyond what ScubaGear itself reads under its own least-privilege scopes.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `CA-2`, `CA-5`, `CA-7`, `CM-6`, `CM-8`, `RA-5`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **CA-2** Security Assessments | Primary | Automated SCuBA assessment produces a complete security evaluation across 7 M365 workloads, normalized into the canonical claim shape. |
| **CA-5** Plan of Action and Milestones | Supporting | Assessment findings feed POA&M generation through the OSCAL pipeline — non-compliant items become POA&M entries with provenance back to the originating ScubaGear finding. |
| **CA-7** Continuous Monitoring | Primary | Scheduled assessment runs (monthly per CONMON.md cadence) provide continuous posture evidence; the Drift Engine (UIAO_110) materializes between-run continuity as `DRIFT-POLICY` findings per UIAO_132 §4. |
| **CM-6** Configuration Settings | Primary | Evaluates 150+ configuration settings against SCuBA baseline policies via OPA/Rego — the canonical per-setting evidence source. |
| **CM-8** Information System Component Inventory | Supporting | Workload enumeration inventories the M365 components under assessment with their current configuration fingerprint. |
| **RA-5** Vulnerability Scanning | Supporting | Configuration misalignments detected by SCuBA serve as vulnerability-adjacent findings on the FedRAMP RFC-0026 traditional pathway. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `powershell-7.4` |
| Runtime pin | `1.5.1` |
| Runner class | `github-hosted` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `interval` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

Paired with the modernization adapter [`scuba`](../scuba/scuba.html) (canon/modernization-registry.yaml), which applies the baseline that ScubaGear assesses. Pin policy: track main + monthly pin bump as Routine Recurring Change per CONMON.md §6.

Advisory mapping (per ADR-043 D4 / UIAO_132 §5, pending FedRAMP RFC-0026 ratification): this adapter satisfies requirement RV5-CA07-VLN (service config scan) and RV5-CA07-CCM (SCuBA-baseline evidence at drop) on the traditional FedRAMP authorization pathway. Drift continuity between point-in-time runs is materialized by the Drift Engine (UIAO_110) as `DRIFT-POLICY` findings per UIAO_132 §4 (BOD 25-01 answer).

ZTMM pillars: identity, applications-and-workloads, data.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-002
- UIAO-CANON-003
- ADR-025 — ScubaGear adapter activation
- ADR-043 — FedRAMP RFC-0026 pathway alignment
- UIAO_110 — Drift Engine (`DRIFT-POLICY` continuity)
- UIAO_132 — Continuous Monitoring Pack
- https://github.com/cisagov/ScubaGear
- https://github.com/FedRAMP/community/discussions/130

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
